Survivor Benefit Plan Open Season, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–263, div. A, title VI, § 643136 Stat. 2634Dec. 23, 2022“(a)Elections by Persons Not Currently Participating in Survivor Benefit Plan.—“(1).—Election of SBP coverageAn eligible retired or former member may elect to participate in the Survivor Benefit Plan during the open season described in subsection (e).“(2).—Eligible retired or former membersFor purposes of paragraph (1), an eligible retired or former member is a member or former member of the uniformed services who, on or before the day before the first day of the open season described in subsection (e)—“(A)is entitled to retired pay; or“(B)would be entitled to retired pay under chapter 1223 of title 10, United States Code (or chapter 67 of such title as in effect before ), but for the fact that such member or former member is under 60 years of age.October 5, 1994“(3)Status under SBP of persons making elections.—“(A).—Standard annuityA person making an election under paragraph (1) by reason of eligibility under paragraph (2)(A) shall be treated for all purposes as providing a standard annuity under the Survivor Benefit Plan.“(B).—Reserve-component annuityA person making an election under paragraph (1) by reason of eligibility under paragraph (2)(B) shall be treated for all purposes as providing a reserve-component annuity under the Survivor Benefit Plan.“(4)Premiums for open season.—“(A).—Premiums to be chargedThe Secretary of Defense shall prescribe in regulations premiums that a person who makes an election under paragraph (1) shall be required to pay for participating in the Survivor Benefit Plan pursuant to the election.“(B).—Amount of premiumsThe total amount of the premiums to be paid by a person under the regulations prescribed under subparagraph (A) shall be equal to the sum of—“(i)the total amount by which the retired pay of the person would have been reduced before the effective date of the election under subsection (d) if the person had elected to participate in the Survivor Benefit Plan (for the same base amount specified in the election) at the first opportunity that was afforded the person to participate under chapter 73 of title 10, United States Code;“(ii)interest on the amount by which the retired pay of the person would have been so reduced, computed from the date on which the retired pay would have been so reduced at such rate or rates and according to such methodology as the Secretary determines reasonable; and“(iii)any additional amount that the Secretary determines necessary to protect the actuarial soundness of the Department of Defense Military Retirement Fund against any increased risk for the fund that is associated with the election.“(C).—Premiums to be credited to retirement fundPremiums paid under the regulations prescribed under subparagraph (A) shall be credited to the Department of Defense Military Retirement Fund.“(b)Elections by Persons Currently Participating in Survivor Benefit Plan.—“(1).—Election of to discontinue SBP participationA person participating in the Survivor Benefit Plan on the day before the first day of the open season described in subsection (e) may elect to discontinue such participation during the open season.“(2)Consent of beneficiaries.—“(A).—In generalExcept as provided in subparagraph (B), a person described in paragraph (1) may not make an election under that paragraph without the concurrence of—“(i)each designated beneficiary of such person under the Survivor Benefit Plan; and“(ii)the spouse of such person, if such person is married.“(B).—Exception when beneficiary unavailableA person may make an election under paragraph (1) without a concurrence required under subparagraph (2) if the person establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary concerned—“(i)that the whereabouts of the spouse or beneficiary, as the case may be, cannot be determined; or“(ii)that, due to exceptional circumstances, requiring the person to seek the consent of the spouse or beneficiary, as the case may be, would otherwise be inappropriate.“(3)Treatment of premiums.—“(A).—Discontinuation of reductions in payAs of the effective date under subsection (d) of an election by a person under paragraph (1), the Secretary concerned shall discontinue the reduction being made in the retired pay of the person arising from participation in the Survivor Benefit Plan or, in the case of a person who has been required to make deposits in the Treasury on account of participation in the Survivor Benefit Plan, that person may discontinue making such deposits effective on such effective date.“(B).—Treatment of previous reductionsA person who makes an election under paragraph (1) is not entitled to a refund of any reduction or deposit described in subparagraph (A) made before such effective date.“(c)Manner of Making Elections.—“(1).—In generalAn election under subsection (a) or (b) shall be made in writing, signed by the person making the election, and received by the Secretary concerned before the end of the open season described in subsection (e).“(2).—ConditionsExcept as provided in paragraph (3), an election under subsection (a) shall be made subject to the same conditions, and with the same opportunities for designation of beneficiaries and specification of base amount, that apply under the Survivor Benefit Plan.“(3).—Election must be voluntaryAn election under subsection (a) or (b) is not effective unless the person making the election declares the election to be voluntary. An election under subsection (a) or (b) to participate or not to participate in the Survivor Benefit Plan may not be required by any court. children.heading: General Rules for Participation in the Plan.—
children.label: a General Rules for Participation in the Plan.—
A
children.text: Persons entitled to retired pay.

children.label: B
children.text: Persons who would be eligible for reserve-component retired pay but for the fact that they are under 60 years of age.

children.heading: .—Standard annuity participants
children.label: A .—Standard annuity participants
children.text: A person who is eligible to participate in the Plan under paragraph (1)(A) and who is married or has a dependent child when he becomes entitled to retired pay, unless he elects (with his spouse’s concurrence, if required under paragraph (3)) not to participate in the Plan before the first day for which he is eligible for that pay.

children.heading: .—Reserve-component annuity participants
children.label: B .—Reserve-component annuity participants
children.text: A person who (i) is eligible to participate in the Plan under paragraph (1)(B), and (ii) is married or has a dependent child when he is notified under that he has completed the years of service required for eligibility for reserve-component retired pay, unless the person elects (with his spouse’s concurrence, if required under paragraph (3)) not to participate in the Plan before the end of the 90-day period beginning on the date on which he receives that notification.section 12731(d) of this title

children.label: B .—Spousal consent for certain elections respecting reserve-component annuity

children.heading: .—Construction with former spouse election provisions
children.label: D .—Construction with former spouse election provisions
children.text: This paragraph does not affect any right or obligation to elect to provide an annuity for a former spouse (or for a former spouse and dependent child) under subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: .—Notice to spouse of election to provide former spouse annuity
children.label: E .—Notice to spouse of election to provide former spouse annuity
children.text: If a married person who is eligible to provide a standard annuity elects to provide an annuity for a former spouse (or for a former spouse and dependent child) under subsection (b)(2), that person’s spouse shall be notified of that election.

children.heading: .—Standard annuity
children.label: A .—Standard annuity
children.text: An election under paragraph (2)(A) is irrevocable if not revoked before the date on which the person first becomes entitled to retired pay.

children.heading: .—Reserve-component annuity
children.label: B .—Reserve-component annuity
children.text: An election under paragraph (2)(B) is irrevocable if not revoked before the end of the 90-day period referred to in that paragraph.

children.heading: Participation by person marrying after retirement, etc.—
children.label: 5 Participation by person marrying after retirement, etc.—

children.heading: .—Election to participate in plan
children.label: A .—Election to participate in plan
children.text: A person who is not married and has no dependent child upon becoming eligible to participate in the Plan but who later marries or acquires a dependent child may elect to participate in the Plan.

children.heading: .—Manner and time of election
children.label: B .—Manner and time of election
children.text: Such an election must be written, signed by the person making the election, and received by the Secretary concerned within one year after the date on which that person marries or acquires that dependent child.

children.heading: .—Limitation on revocation of election
children.label: C .—Limitation on revocation of election
children.text: Such an election may not be revoked except in accordance with subsection (b)(3).

children.heading: .—Effective date of election
children.label: D .—Effective date of election
children.text: The election is effective as of the first day of the first calendar month following the month in which the election is received by the Secretary concerned.

children.heading: .—Designation if rcsbp election
children.label: E .—Designation if rcsbp election
children.text: In the case of a person providing a reserve-component annuity, such an election shall include a designation under subsection (e).

children.label: B .—Effect of election on retired pay

children.heading: .—Construction with former spouse election provisions
children.label: E .—Construction with former spouse election provisions
children.text: This paragraph does not affect any right or obligation to elect to provide an annuity to a former spouse under subsection (b).

children.heading: Insurable Interest and Former Spouse Coverage.—
children.label: b Insurable Interest and Former Spouse Coverage.—

children.heading: Coverage for person with insurable interest.—
children.label: 1 Coverage for person with insurable interest.—

children.heading: .—General rule
children.label: A .—General rule
children.text: A person who is not married and does not have a dependent child upon becoming eligible to participate in the Plan may elect to provide an annuity under the Plan to a natural person with an insurable interest in that person. In the case of a person providing a reserve-component annuity, such an election shall include a designation under subsection (e).

children.heading: .—Termination of coverage
children.label: B .—Termination of coverage
children.text: An election under subparagraph (A) for a beneficiary who is not the former spouse of the person providing the annuity may be terminated. Any such termination shall be made by a participant by the submission to the Secretary concerned of a request to discontinue participation in the Plan, and such participation in the Plan shall be discontinued effective on the first day of the first month following the month in which the request is received by the Secretary concerned. Effective on such date, the Secretary concerned shall discontinue the reduction being made in such person’s retired pay on account of participation in the Plan or, in the case of a person who has been required to make deposits in the Treasury on account of participation in the Plan, such person may discontinue making such deposits effective on such date.

children.heading: .—Form for discontinuation
children.label: C .—Form for discontinuation
children.text: A request under subparagraph (B) to discontinue participation in the Plan shall be in such form and shall contain such information as may be required under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense.

children.heading: .—Withdrawal of request for discontinuation
children.label: D .—Withdrawal of request for discontinuation
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall furnish promptly to each person who submits a request under subparagraph (B) to discontinue participation in the Plan a written statement of the advantages and disadvantages of participating in the Plan and the possible disadvantages of discontinuing participation. A person may withdraw the request to discontinue participation if withdrawn within 30 days after having been submitted to the Secretary concerned.

children.heading: .—Consequences of discontinuation
children.label: E .—Consequences of discontinuation
children.text: Once participation is discontinued, benefits may not be paid in conjunction with the earlier participation in the Plan and premiums paid may not be refunded. Participation in the Plan may not later be resumed except through a qualified election under paragraph (5) of subsection (a) or under subparagraph (G) of this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Vitiation of election by disability retiree who dies of disability-related cause
children.label: F .—Vitiation of election by disability retiree who dies of disability-related cause
i
children.text: an election made by the member under paragraph (1) to provide an annuity under the Plan to any person other than a dependent of that member (as defined in ) is vitiated; andsection 1072(2) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the amounts by which the member’s retired pay was reduced under shall be refunded and paid to the person to whom the annuity under the Plan would have been paid pursuant to such election.section 1452 of this title

children.heading: Election of new beneficiary upon death of previous beneficiary.—
children.label: G Election of new beneficiary upon death of previous beneficiary.—

children.heading: .—Authority for election
children.label: i .—Authority for election
children.text: If the reason for discontinuation in the Plan is the death of the beneficiary, the participant in the Plan may elect a new beneficiary. Any such beneficiary must be a natural person with an insurable interest in the participant. Such an election may be made only during the 180-day period beginning on the date of the death of the previous beneficiary.

children.heading: .—Procedures
children.label: ii .—Procedures
children.text: Such an election shall be in writing, signed by the participant, and made in such form and manner as the Secretary concerned may prescribe. Such an election shall be effective the first day of the first month following the month in which the election is received by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Vitiation of election by participant who dies within two years of election
children.label: iii .—Vitiation of election by participant who dies within two years of election
I
children.text: the election is vitiated; and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount by which the person’s retired pay was reduced under that is attributable to the election shall be paid in a lump sum to the person who would have been the deceased person’s beneficiary under the vitiated election if the deceased person had died after the end of such two-year period.section 1452 of this title

children.heading: Former spouse coverage upon becoming a participant in the plan.—
children.label: 2 Former spouse coverage upon becoming a participant in the plan.—

children.heading: .—General rule
children.label: A .—General rule
children.text: A person who has a former spouse upon becoming eligible to participate in the Plan may elect to provide an annuity to that former spouse.

children.heading: .—Effect of former spouse election on spouse or dependent child
children.label: B .—Effect of former spouse election on spouse or dependent child
children.text: In the case of a person with a spouse or a dependent child, such an election prevents payment of an annuity to that spouse or child (other than a child who is a beneficiary under an election under paragraph (4)), including payment under subsection (d).

children.heading: .—Designation if more than one former spouse
children.label: C .—Designation if more than one former spouse
children.text: If there is more than one former spouse, the person shall designate which former spouse is to be provided the annuity.

children.heading: .—Designation if rcsbp election
children.label: D .—Designation if rcsbp election
children.text: In the case of a person providing a reserve-component annuity, such an election shall include a designation under subsection (e).

children.heading: Former spouse coverage by persons already participating in plan.—
children.label: 3 Former spouse coverage by persons already participating in plan.—
I
children.text: who is a participant in the Plan and is providing coverage for a spouse or a spouse and child (even though there is no beneficiary currently eligible for such coverage), and

children.label: II
children.text: who has a former spouse who was not that person’s former spouse when that person became eligible to participate in the Plan,

children.heading: .—Termination of previous coverage
children.label: ii .—Termination of previous coverage
children.text: Any such election terminates any previous coverage under the Plan.

children.heading: .—Manner and time of election
children.label: iii .—Manner and time of election
children.text: Any such election must be written, signed by the person making the election, and received by the Secretary concerned within one year after the date of the decree of divorce, dissolution, or annulment.

children.label: i
children.text: the person was married to that former spouse for at least one year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: that former spouse is the parent of issue by that marriage.

children.heading: .—Irrevocability, etc
children.label: C .—Irrevocability, etc
children.text: An election under this paragraph may not be revoked except in accordance with . This paragraph does not provide the authority to change a designation previously made under subsection (e).section 1450(f) of this title

children.heading: .—Notice to spouse
children.label: D .—Notice to spouse
children.text: If a person who is married makes an election to provide an annuity to a former spouse under this paragraph, that person’s spouse shall be notified of the election.

children.label: i
children.text: the first day of the first month following the month in which the election is received by the Secretary concerned; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a person required (as described in ) to make the election by reason of a court order or filing the date of which is after , the first day of the first month which begins after the date of that court order or filing.section 1450(f)(3)(B) of this titleOctober 16, 1998

children.heading: .—Former spouse and child coverage
children.label: 4 .—Former spouse and child coverage
children.text: A person who elects to provide an annuity for a former spouse under paragraph (2) or (3) may, at the time of the election, elect to provide coverage under that annuity for both the former spouse and a dependent child, if the child resulted from the person’s marriage to that former spouse.

children.heading: .—Disclosure of whether election of former spouse coverage is required
children.label: 5 .—Disclosure of whether election of former spouse coverage is required
A
children.text: whether the election is being made pursuant to the requirements of a court order; or

children.label: B
children.text: whether the election is being made pursuant to a written agreement previously entered into voluntarily by such person as a part of, or incident to, a proceeding of divorce, dissolution, or annulment and (if so) whether such voluntary written agreement has been incorporated in, or ratified or approved by, a court order.

children.heading: .—Special needs trusts for sole benefit of certain dependent children
children.label: 6 .—Special needs trusts for sole benefit of certain dependent children
children.text: A person who has established a supplemental or special needs trust under subparagraph (A) or (C) of section 1917(d)(4) of the Social Security Act () for the sole benefit of a dependent child considered disabled under section 1614(a)(3) of that Act () who is incapable of self-support because of mental or physical incapacity may elect to provide an annuity to that supplemental or special needs trust.42 U.S.C. 1396p(d)(4)42 U.S.C. 1382c(a)(3)

children.heading: Effect of death of former spouse beneficiary.—
children.label: 7 Effect of death of former spouse beneficiary.—

children.heading: .—Termination of participation in plan
children.label: A .—Termination of participation in plan
children.text: A person who elects to provide an annuity to a former spouse under paragraph (2) or (3) and whose former spouse subsequently dies is no longer a participant in the Plan, effective on the date of death of the former spouse.

children.heading: .—Authority for election of new spouse beneficiary
children.label: B .—Authority for election of new spouse beneficiary

children.heading: .—Married on the date of death of former spouse
children.label: i .—Married on the date of death of former spouse
children.text: A person who is married at the time of the death of the former spouse beneficiary may elect to provide coverage to that person’s spouse. Such an election must be received by the Secretary concerned within one year after the date of death of the former spouse beneficiary.

children.heading: .—Marriage after death of former spouse beneficiary
children.label: ii .—Marriage after death of former spouse beneficiary
children.text: A person who is not married at the time of the death of the former spouse beneficiary and who later marries may elect to provide spouse coverage. Such an election must be received by the Secretary concerned within one year after the date on which that person marries.

children.label: i
children.text: An election under subparagraph (B)(i) is effective as of the first day of the first calendar month following the death of the former spouse beneficiary.

children.label: ii
children.text: An election under subparagraph (B)(ii) is effective as of the first day of the first calendar month following the month in which the election is received by the Secretary concerned.

children.heading: .—Level of coverage
children.label: D .—Level of coverage
children.text: A person making an election under subparagraph (B) may not reduce the base amount previously elected.

children.heading: .—Procedures
children.label: E .—Procedures
children.text: An election under this paragraph shall be in writing, signed by the participant, and made in such form and manner as the Secretary concerned may prescribe.

children.heading: .—Irrevocability
children.label: F .—Irrevocability
children.text: An election under this paragraph is irrevocable.

children.heading: .—Persons on Temporary Disability Retired List
children.label: c .—Persons on Temporary Disability Retired List
children.text: The application of the Plan to a person whose name is on the temporary disability retired list terminates when his name is removed from that list and he is no longer entitled to disability retired pay.

children.heading: Coverage for Survivors of Members Who Die on Active Duty.—
children.label: d Coverage for Survivors of Members Who Die on Active Duty.—
i
children.text: becoming eligible to receive retired pay;

children.label: ii
children.text: qualifying for retired pay except that the member has not applied for or been granted that pay; or

children.label: iii
children.text: completing 20 years of active service but before the member is eligible to retire as a commissioned officer because the member has not completed 10 years of active commissioned service; or

children.label: B
children.text: a member not described in subparagraph (A) who dies in line of duty while on active duty.

children.heading: .—Dependent children
children.label: 2 .—Dependent children
children.text: In the case of a member described in paragraph (1), the Secretary concerned shall pay an annuity under this subchapter to the member’s dependent children under subsection (a)(2) or (a)(4) of as applicable.section 1450 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: may not pay an annuity under paragraph (1) or (2); but

children.label: B
children.text: shall pay an annuity to that former spouse as if the member had been a participant in the Plan and had made an election under subsection (b) to provide an annuity to the former spouse, or in accordance with that election, as the case may be, if the Secretary receives a written request from the former spouse concerned that the election be deemed to have been made in the same manner as provided in .section 1450(f)(3) of this title

children.heading: .—Priority
children.label: 4 .—Priority
children.text: An annuity that may be provided under this subsection shall be provided in preference to an annuity that may be provided under any other provision of this subchapter on account of service of the same member.

children.heading: .—Computation
children.label: 5 .—Computation
children.text: The amount of an annuity under this subsection is computed under .section 1451(c) of this title

children.heading: .—Annuity for dependent
children.label: A .—Annuity for dependent
children.text: In the case of a member described in paragraph (1) who dies after , the Secretary concerned may, if no other annuity is payable on behalf of the member under this subchapter, pay an annuity to a natural person who has an insurable interest in such member as if the annuity were elected by the member under subsection (b)(1). The Secretary concerned may pay such an annuity under this paragraph only in the case of a person who is a dependent of that member (as defined in ).November 23, 2003section 1072(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Computation of annuity
children.label: B .—Computation of annuity
children.text: An annuity under this subparagraph shall be computed under as if the member had retired for total disability on the date of death with reductions as specified under , as applicable to the ages of the member and the natural person with an insurable interest.section 1451(b) of this titlesection 1452(c) of this title

children.heading: .—Designation for Commencement of Reserve-Component Annuity
children.label: e .—Designation for Commencement of Reserve-Component Annuity

children.text: the day after the date of his death; or
the 60th anniversary of his birth.

children.heading: Coverage of Survivors of Persons Dying When or Before Eligible To Elect Reserve-Component Annuity.—
children.label: f Coverage of Survivors of Persons Dying When or Before Eligible To Elect Reserve-Component Annuity.—
i
children.text: before being notified under that he has completed the years of service required for eligibility for reserve-component retired pay; orsection 12731(d) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: during the 90-day period beginning on the date he receives notification under that he has completed the years of service required for eligibility for reserve-component retired pay if he had not made an election under subsection (a)(2)(B) to participate in the Plan; orsection 12731(d) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is a member of a reserve component not described in subparagraph (A) and dies from an injury or illness incurred or aggravated in the line of duty during inactive-duty training.

children.heading: .—Annuity when no eligible surviving spouse
children.label: A .—Annuity when no eligible surviving spouse
children.text: In the case of a person described in paragraph (1), the Secretary concerned shall pay an annuity under this subchapter to the dependent children of that person under as applicable.section 1450(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Optional annuity when there is an eligible surviving spouse
children.label: B .—Optional annuity when there is an eligible surviving spouse
children.text: The Secretary may pay an annuity under this subchapter to the dependent children of a person described in paragraph (1) under , if applicable, instead of paying an annuity to the surviving spouse under paragraph (1), if the Secretary concerned, in consultation with the surviving spouse, determines it appropriate to provide an annuity for the dependent children under this paragraph instead of an annuity for the surviving spouse under paragraph (1).section 1450(a)(3) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: may not pay an annuity under paragraph (1) or (2); but

children.label: B
children.text: shall pay an annuity to that former spouse as if the person had been a participant in the Plan and had made an election under subsection (b) to provide an annuity to the former spouse, or in accordance with that election, as the case may be, if the Secretary receives a written request from the former spouse concerned that the election be deemed to have been made in the same manner as provided in .section 1450(f)(3) of this title

children.heading: .—Computation
children.label: 4 .—Computation
children.text: The amount of an annuity under this subsection is computed under .section 1451(c) of this title

children.heading: .—Deemed election to provide an annuity for dependent
children.label: 5 .—Deemed election to provide an annuity for dependent
children.text: Paragraph (6) of subsection (d) shall apply in the case of a member described in paragraph (1) who dies after , when no other annuity is payable on behalf of the member under this subchapter.November 23, 2003

children.heading: Election To Increase Coverage Upon Remarriage.—
children.label: g Election To Increase Coverage Upon Remarriage.—
A
children.text: who is a participant in the Plan and is providing coverage under subsection (a) for a spouse or a spouse and child, but at less than the maximum level; and

children.label: B
children.text: who remarries,

children.heading: .—Payment required
children.label: 2 .—Payment required
children.text: Such an election shall be contingent on the person paying to the United States the amount determined under paragraph (3) plus interest on such amount at a rate determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount that would have been withheld from such person’s retired pay under if the higher level of coverage had been in effect from the time the person became a participant in the Plan; andsection 1452 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of such person’s retired pay actually withheld.

children.heading: .—Manner of making election
children.label: 4 .—Manner of making election
children.text: An election under paragraph (1) shall be made in such manner as the Secretary shall prescribe and shall become effective upon receipt of the payment required by paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Disposition of payments
children.label: 5 .—Disposition of payments
children.text: A payment received under this subsection by the Secretary of Defense shall be deposited into the Department of Defense Military Retirement Fund. Any other payment received under this subsection shall be deposited in the Treasury as miscellaneous receipts.
